---
title: "Plan ‚Äî global subsample mode (`--total-count` / `--ratio`)"
author: "Yun Jiang"
date: "2026-07-23"
format:
  html:
    html-math-method: mathjax
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <link rel="icon" href="data:image/svg+xml,<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 100 100'><text y='.9em' font-size='90'>üß¨</text></svg>">
---

## Objective

Add a **global** subsample mode to `sam-subsampler` that pools every unique qname
across all references into one reservoir ‚Äî i.e. it does **not** care which
reference a read maps to, unlike the existing per-reference `--count`. Two new,
mutually-exclusive flags:

- `--total-count <N>` ‚Äî tag exactly `min(N, U)` unique reads, where `U` = total
  unique mapped qnames.
- `--ratio <F>` ‚Äî tag `round(U ¬∑ F)` unique reads, `F ‚àà (0, 1]`.

Both are mutually exclusive with `--count` and `--config`. The output is still the
full file (tag-in-place, not filtering); selected reads gain the aux tag.

## Why this works cheaply

Pass 2 (`bam_io::tag_and_write`, `src/bam_io.rs:74`) tags a record purely by qname
membership in a `HashSet` ‚Äî it is reference-agnostic. So the change is confined to
the **selection layer**; pass 1 / pass 2 are untouched. The qname-dedup bias fix
(one qname = one selection unit; all its records tagged) is preserved and extended
to qnames that appear on more than one reference.

## Scope (files) ‚Äî as implemented

1. `src/config.rs` ‚Äî added `GlobalTotal(u32)` / `GlobalRatio(f64)` to
   `SubsamplePlan`; kept `count_for` exhaustive (new arms return `0`, documented
   as bypassed by the dispatcher). +2 unit tests.
2. `src/selection.rs` ‚Äî new `pub fn select()` dispatcher; private
   `pooled_sorted_unique()` (flatten ‚Üí dedup across references ‚Üí sort),
   `global_reservoir()` (seeded RNG + one `reservoir_select`), and
   `ratio_to_target()` (`round(U¬∑F)` clamped to `[1, U]`, `0` when `U == 0`). +8
   unit tests + 1 proptest.
3. `src/cli.rs` ‚Äî `--total-count` / `--ratio` flags; struct-level
   `group = ArgGroup::new("selection_mode").multiple(false)` + per-field
   `group = "selection_mode"` (verified clap-derive pattern from
   [docs.rs/clap](https://docs.rs/clap/latest/src/clap/_derive/_tutorial.rs.html));
   `validate_ratio()` (finite, `(0, 1]`). +8 tests.
4. `src/lib.rs` ‚Äî `build_plan` maps the new flags; `try_run` calls
   `selection::select`.
5. `tests/integration.rs` ‚Äî `run_pipeline` uses `select`; +7 end-to-end tests.
6. `README.md` ‚Äî feature blurb, CLI table, usage, algorithm, reproducibility, test
   count (‚Üí 80).

## Design decisions

- **Both count and ratio** (user-chosen via `AskUserQuestion`). Ratio target =
  `round(U¬∑F)` clamped to `[1, U]`; `U == 0 ‚áí 0`.
- **Ratio basis = the true deduped pool length** (`pool.len()`), not the sum of
  per-reference set sizes (which would double-count qnames on >1 reference). This
  is why the pool is built once (`pooled_sorted_unique`) before the target is
  derived.
- **Single `SubsamplePlan` enum** (KISS) over a nested-enum split.
- **Mutual exclusivity** via clap `ArgGroup`, replacing pairwise `conflicts_with`.
- **Reproducibility**: pooled qnames sorted before sampling; one seeded RNG. Same
  chromosome-shift caveat as the per-ref path applies to the global draw.

## Verification ‚Äî passed

```sh
pixi run cargo fmt --all -- --check          # clean
pixi run cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings   # clean
pixi run cargo test                          # 80 tests pass (66 unit + 14 integration)
```

Exit criteria met: fmt/clippy clean; all tests green; `--total-count N` tags
`min(N, U)`; `--ratio F` tags `round(U¬∑F)`; mutual-exclusion + ratio bounds
behave; existing per-ref behavior unchanged.

## Status

**Done.** See the companion report `20260723-194418_global-subsample.report.qmd`.
Changes are uncommitted in the working tree, ready for review.
